title: 2 dead, several trapped as fire breaks out at Noida residential building
content: A fire erupted in a Noida residential building on Wednesday. Two individuals tragically lost their lives in the Mamura village incident. Several residents are feared trapped inside the burning structure. Local residents captured videos showing thick smoke billowing from the building. People on the terrace used a ladder to escape the dangerous situation.

title: Nashik highway horror: New video shows attacker hanging onto moving car as family tries to escape
content: A disturbing new video captures the moment a man clings precariously to the speeding SUV of a tourist family. According to their claims, the incident began after a woman protested against harassment. During the harrowing chase, the attackers reportedly rammed their vehicle and broke windows while the couple's young daughters watched helplessly. Law enforcement is now probing this serious case that unfolded on the highway.

title: 'We're coming for you': Lamine Yamal sends Spain World Cup final warning
content: Spain's teenage sensation Lamine Yamal expressed confidence after reaching the FIFA World Cup 2026 final.  He posted a message indicating their intention to win the championship.  Spain secured their place by defeating France 2-0 in the semifinal match.  The team celebrated their victory while shifting focus to the upcoming championship game.  They now await their opponent in the final match to be held in New York.

title: Supriya Sule rules out NDA switch, says NCP (SP) yet to decide on Delimitation Bill
content: Supriya Sule denied her party would support the Delimitation Bill without discussion.  She stated the NCP (SP) remains firmly aligned with the INDIA alliance.  Discussions about the bill will occur within the INDIA bloc after its introduction.  Recent meetings with Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis fueled merger speculation.  However, NCP leaders have refuted claims of any merger talks.

title: IBM CEO's statement that wiped away $70 bn has added millions for cybersecurity firm
content: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna noted advanced AI concerns are causing companies to pause technology deals. Businesses are now rethinking cybersecurity spending priorities as AI evolves rapidly. IBM's preliminary second-quarter results missed Wall Street expectations, causing shares to fall. Cybersecurity stocks, however, surged as investors anticipated increased spending on protection. Krishna acknowledged IBM did not adapt quickly enough to market shifts.

title: 'In solidarity with Wangchuk': CJP announces 1-day mass hunger strike on July 16
content: The Cockroach Janata Party will hold a hunger strike on July 16 supporting Sonam Wangchuk. Wangchuk's health is declining on his eighteenth day of fasting. He has lost significant muscle mass and is experiencing immense pain. A court petition seeks to force-feed Wangchuk and move him to a hospital. This intervention is deemed necessary to preserve his life and prevent further deterioration.

title: A pothole-mukt Bharat: Here’s the answer
content: Indian cities face recurring costs from pothole repairs on asphalt roads. Concrete roads offer a more durable and cost-effective long-term solution. Mumbai is leading this transition with extensive cement concrete road projects. While upfront costs are higher, concrete roads reduce maintenance and associated economic losses. This shift aims for sustainable infrastructure and a pothole-free nation.

title: 'I'm a bit tipsy': Sinner's honest Wimbledon Ball confession wins hearts — Watch
content: Jannik Sinner charmed guests at the Champions' Ball with candid remarks. He admitted to being slightly tipsy while discussing his mother's stress. Sinner also humorously shared his repeated motorcycle license test failures. The world number one successfully defended his Wimbledon crown recently. This victory marked his fifth Grand Slam title and hundredth match win.

title: Jeff Bezos’ wife Lauren Sánchez leads $100M investment to transform 8 US cities
content: Lauren Sanchez Bezos is directing significant Bezos Earth Fund investments. The fund is transforming urban spaces into green community hubs. This initiative aims to connect people and nature within cities. The Bezos Earth Fund also supports marine protection and AI climate solutions. Overall, the fund continues its commitment to climate and nature initiatives.

title: Doctors report rise in Influenza A, Covid-19 and H1N1 this monsoon, urge caution
content: Respiratory infections like Influenza A and COVID-19 are increasing during the monsoon. Doctors advise awareness and proper medical treatment for these common infections. Vulnerable groups should seek prompt medical evaluation if symptoms worsen. Unnecessary antibiotic use contributes to antimicrobial resistance, experts warn. Simple precautions and timely testing help prevent severe complications.

title: Anthony Davis sells his Bel Air mansion for $32 million
content: NBA superstar Anthony Davis has made headlines by selling his extravagant Bel Air mansion for a whopping thirty-two million dollars. This marks a significant transition for the athlete as he leaves Los Angeles following the conclusion of his Lakers contract. After investing in extensive renovations, Davis had initially listed the property for nearly forty million dollars, having bought it in twenty twenty-one for thirty-one million dollars.

title: How Mumbai's expanding Metro network is changing homebuyer preferences
content: Metro connectivity is increasingly influencing property values in India's largest cities. Proximity to metro stations now significantly impacts apartment prices more than traditional addresses. Mumbai's property market shows a clear shift towards connectivity-adjusted value for buyers. Gurgaon's transformation into a real estate hotspot exemplifies this transit-driven appreciation. Understanding future metro routes is becoming crucial for property investment decisions.

title: Cheaper cars & whisky: India set to win big as FTA with UK kicks in
content: With its implementation, the trade deal will become the sixth free trade agreement brought into force during the Narendra Modi government's tenure. India has previously implemented similar agreements with Mauritius, the UAE, Australia, the European Free Trade Association (EFTA) and Oman.

title: Captaincy, coaching, trade talk to headline Mumbai Indians' season review next week
content: Mumbai Indians' top brass will meet in England next week for a season review that could shape the franchise's future. Captaincy, Mahela Jayawardene's coaching role and Hardik Pandya's trade situation will dominate discussions.  The five-time champions finished fourth in 2025 and a disappointing ninth in the 2026 edition.

title: Why Taslima Nasreen's Kolkata return is a powder keg for Bengal politics
content: Taslima Nasreen's upcoming return to Kolkata after 20 years sparks intense political discussions. The Trinamool Congress and Bharatiya Janata Party both voiced their strong opinions regarding her visit. Exiled since 2007, Nasreen is set to participate in an anti-fundamentalism literary event this August. Her previous works and the circumstances of her exile have sent shockwaves through conversations about free speech and the intersection with religious beliefs in West Bengal.

title: Train smarter, fight better: How AI‑powered simulations are making better soldiers
content: Modern armed forces are increasingly adopting AI-enhanced simulators for training. These advanced systems significantly reduce the high costs and dangers of live-fire exercises. Simulators also minimize wear and tear on expensive military equipment and systems. They offer personalized and continuously evolving training scenarios for soldiers and pilots. This technological shift makes military preparation more efficient and effective for future conflicts.

title: Why the Sea of Azov is the new flashpoint in the Russia-Ukraine war
content: In a heated exchange, Russia has labeled Ukraine's recent maritime strikes in the Sea of Azov as acts of terrorism, jeopardizing a crucial grain export route essential for its economy. While Ukraine asserts its attacks focus on military targets, the situation escalates as maritime drone operations increase, targeting Russian vessels. Russia's priority remains to safeguard its grain harvest and maintain robust supply lines during the conflict.

title: Soni Razdan, Shabana Azmi and Swara Bhasker rally behind Sonam Wangchuk
content: Film personalities are urging activist Sonam Wangchuk to end his hunger strike. Concerns are growing over his deteriorating health after weeks of fasting. Shabana Azmi and Soni Razdan have appealed for his well-being and continued leadership. Other celebrities and writers also joined calls for him to end the fast. Wangchuk is protesting alleged NEET examination irregularities and demanding minister's resignation.

title: How Spain ended 'Dictator' Mbappe's reign and blew France apart
content: Until they met Spain, Mbappe and Co were tearing defences apart with disdain, but from Spain they finally learnt the meaning of pain. Today’s OFFSIDE explains how Spain took France’s imperial swagger and tore it Bona-part.

title: 'Hindus and Sikhs of Punjab are watching the movie together,' says 'Satluj' director Honey Trehan
content: Director Honey Trehan denies his film 'Satluj' is propaganda and claims it fosters unity. He states Hindus and Sikhs watched the movie together peacefully in Punjab. Trehan argues the government's intervention led to the film's removal from the platform. The film was released without completing the mandatory certification process, according to officials. 'Satluj' is inspired by human rights activist Jaswant Singh Khalra's work.

title: IPL coaches dominate England's shortlist in race to find McCullum's successor
content: England's cricket board seeks a new Test coach with IPL experience. Several accomplished franchise coaches are now leading contenders for the role. Andy Flower and Stephen Fleming are among the prominent names considered. The board aims for a long-term appointment before next year's Ashes. An interim coach might lead the team for the Pakistan series.

title: 'May die in 2 days, lost 8.5kg': Plea seeks 'force-feeding' of Sonam Wangchuk
content: A plea seeks court intervention to force-feed activist Sonam Wangchuk, whose hunger strike has entered its eighteenth day. Concerns are growing over his deteriorating health as he has reportedly lost significant weight. The petition urges authorities to shift him to a government hospital for medical intervention. It argues that saving his life is of paramount importance for the nation.

title: England vs Argentina: Rivalry and revenge at the FIFA World Cup
content: The intense Argentina and England football rivalry spans several World Cup matches.  Key moments include the 1966 Rattin expulsion and Maradona's 1986 goals.  David Beckham faced controversy after a 1998 red card against Argentina.  England secured a narrow victory in the 2002 group stage.  These encounters highlight enduring historical grievances and national pride.

title: FIFA WC: Why FBI labelled Argentina vs England semi-final the 'highest-risk' match
content: England and Argentina's World Cup semifinal is the tournament's highest-risk match.  Extraordinary security measures are in place for this intense football rivalry.  Decades of political tensions and historic flashpoints fuel concerns about fan behaviour.  Officials are implementing separate entry gates to minimise potential confrontations.  Authorities are ensuring this sensitive fixture remains confined to the pitch.

title: Class 7 student attempts suicide after 'punishment' by teacher in Bengaluru
content: A 13-year-old student is critical after allegedly attempting suicide following school punishment. His family claims a teacher subjected him to mental harassment and humiliation. The boy had previously expressed his desire not to study at the school. Parents allege caste-based abuse by students and teachers at the institution. Authorities are awaiting a formal complaint to initiate an investigation into the incident.

title: Sam Neill's family opts for private New Zealand funeral
content: In a heartfelt tribute to legendary actor Sam Neill, his family has decided to hold a private funeral service in New Zealand, reflecting his lifelong wish for seclusion and connection to his homeland. The iconic star, famed for his role in Jurassic Park, passed away at the age of 78, with only close family and friends expected to attend this intimate gathering.

title: China's economic growth slows to weakest pace in three years despite export boom
content: China's economic growth slowed in the second quarter, impacted by weak domestic demand. Strong exports, particularly in electric vehicles and AI, provided some support. Consumer spending and investment remain subdued amid property market uncertainty. The nation faces challenges balancing high-tech growth with job creation. Officials aim for higher quality growth and a stronger domestic market.

title: No red lights, still red every evening: Why Akshardham-Noida road chokes daily
content: Traffic slows on the Akshardham-Noida corridor despite its design for quick movement.  Small interruptions like vehicle stops and lane changes cause traffic to break.  Commuter traffic, metro pickups, and buses compete for road edge space.  This leads to delays, increased pollution, and dangerous driving conditions.  Designated bays and stricter enforcement are suggested solutions for this recurring problem.

title: 'Don’t settle for India A, aim for 100 Tests': Nehra’s advice to Ashok Sharma
content: Ashok Sharma earned his India call-up after a challenging journey and brother's encouragement. He will now buy a gold chain after his international debut. Sharma focuses on his bowling strengths and drawing from IPL experiences. Gujarat Titans coach Ashish Nehra urged him to aim for 100 Test matches. His elder brother sacrificed his own cricket dreams to support Ashok's ambition.

title: Sensex jumps over 500 points, Nifty above 24,200: Top reasons driving stock market rally
content: Dalal Street experienced a strong rebound on Wednesday, reversing previous session losses.  Improving tech sentiments and a firmer rupee boosted investor confidence significantly.  Geopolitical developments and global cues also supported the market's upward movement.  Foreign investors turned net buyers in July, contributing to the rally.  Asian markets and technology stocks saw a notable rebound after US inflation data.

title: Turkey's S-400 gamble: How a Gulf sale could redraw West Asia's air defence map
content: In a significant development, Turkey is exploring the sale of its Russian S-400 air defence systems to the UAE. This transaction could reshape the air defence dynamics in West Asia, potentially paving the way for Turkey's return to the F-35 program and alleviating sanctions. However, Russia's consent remains vital for this delicate arms deal amidst rising regional tensions and shifting security priorities.

title: Rising heat leaves 57M hectares of tropical forests unable to photosynthesise efficiently
content: Tropical forests are experiencing critical heat thresholds impacting photosynthesis. Millions of hectares already exceed average critical temperature limits. Excessive heat reduces the efficiency of trees capturing atmospheric carbon. Projections indicate significant future increases in heat-stressed forest areas. Some species may adapt, but adaptation speed remains uncertain.

title: Aamir Khan responds to ‘love jihad’ controversy amid fatwa
content: Aamir Khan denies being a 'love jihad' ambassador, emphasizing his family's inclusive interfaith marriages. He clarified that none of his partners converted their religion during their civil unions. His recent marriage to Gauri Spratt has sparked political and religious debate online. Maulana Ibrahim Hussain reportedly issued a fatwa against Khan for his interfaith marriage. Khan and Spratt have known each other for nearly 25 years and reconnected recently.

title: Gold price prediction: Where are gold & silver headed? Check outlook for today
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are likely to see a constructive outlook in the near-term, while silver is supported for the long-term, says Vedika Narvekar, Research Analyst - Commodities & Currencies, Anand Rathi Shares and Stock Brokers.

title: 'I was trying to ...': Axar Patel reveals what went wrong in T20Is after ODI heroics
content: Axar Patel admitted to overhitting the ball during the T20 series.  He focused on timing over power for his match-winning ODI performance.  His all-round display guided India to a six-wicket victory in the first ODI.  Captain Shubman Gill and Washington Sundar also contributed valuable runs.  India now lead the three-match series one-nil against England.

title: After 18 years, Bangladesh-born author-in-exile Taslima Nasrin to return to Kolkata
content: Taslima Nasrin is making her long-anticipated return to Kolkata after an exile spanning eighteen years, forced to leave due to protests in November 2007. Overjoyed to come back to what she calls home, she will participate in a literary meet. The event features a cultural program honoring the author, accompanied by increased security to maintain order and prevent unrest.

title: Agni, Akash, Prithvi & more: How a 1983 Kalam‑led project forged India's missile mastery
content: India's Integrated Guided Missile Development Programme began in 1983. This initiative aimed for domestic missile technology and strategic autonomy. Five distinct missile projects were conceived to meet operational needs. These projects transformed India into a credible missile manufacturer. The programme achieved significant technological independence in missile development.

title: Man's fast ruined by fungus-contaminated juice; court awards Rs 70,000
content: A Himachal Pradesh consumer commission found Dabur India liable for selling contaminated juice. The company must pay one lakh rupees for compensation and costs. A man discovered fungus in his Real Fruit Power Mosambi juice pack. He suffered emotional and spiritual distress while observing a religious fast. The commission cited manufacturing defects and negligence in its ruling.

title: Six times, same result! Yamal continues to be Mbappe's knockout nemesis
content: Lamine Yamal continued his flawless knockout streak, overcoming Kylian Mbappé as Spain triumphed over France 2-0 in the FIFA World Cup 2026 semifinal. This impressive win guarantees Spain a spot in the finals. Remarkably, Yamal has now achieved six consecutive victories against Mbappé in knockout rounds, showcasing the young talent's resilience in high-stakes matches.

title: ‘If you don’t want E20, you can buy 100% petrol, but pay more’: Nitin Gadkari
content: Union minister Nitin Gadkari denied personal gain from the ethanol blending program. He stated that allegations of conflict of interest are politically motivated and lack substance. Gadkari emphasized his long-standing support for alternative fuels to reduce pollution and import dependence. He clarified that his sons' business has a minimal share in the ethanol sector. The minister also highlighted the program's historical backing from previous governments.

title: NHL trade rumor: Patrick Kane linked to Edmonton Oilers in free agency
content: Patrick Kane is a potential free agent target for the Edmonton Oilers.  The veteran winger is reportedly considering signing with a fourth NHL franchise.  Detroit Red Wings attempted to retain Kane with a contract offer.  Edmonton has cap space and seeks to strengthen its roster for 2026-27.  Kane brings championship experience and offensive prowess to contenders.

title: Supreme Court refuses to ‘short-circuit’ procedure, declines PIL on Prophet Muhammad remarks
content: Do not sensationalise sensitive matters, file complaint with police: Supreme Court reacts to oral plea about alleged remarks by social media influencer Nazia Elahi Khan

title: Supreme Court rejects plea to curb reels, podcasts; warns against politicising Article 32
content: The Supreme Court Bench was hearing a petition against the online circulation of allegedly derogatory comments posted by an influencer against Prophet Muhammed

title: Staines murder case: Supreme Court gives Odisha govt. one month to consider Dara Singh's remission plea
content: Dara Singh also sought a direction to the State government to consider his case in accordance with the guidelines for premature release of life convicts issued in 2022 in the three cases in which he was convicted

title: Odisha panel clears early release of prime convict in murder of Graham Staines
content: Odisha State Sentence Review Board has recommended Dara Singh’s release on the grounds of ‘good behaviour’

title: Supriya Sule calls delimitation row a ‘storm in a teacup’, denies backing proposal
content: Along with a 50% increase in seats for all States, there should be clarity about the process as well, says NCP (SP) leader Supriya Sule

title: Abhishek Banerjee submits voice sample before Bidhannagar City Court
content: Justice Saugata Bhattacharyya of Calcutta High Court had directed Abhishek Banerjee to appear before before the court and submit his voice sample by July 15

title: Congress invites Mamata to July 21 event in Kolkata, asks her to admit quitting party was 'mistake'
content: The July 21, 1993 movement was organised under the banner of the Youth Congress and that its association with the Congress could not be erased says, West Bengal Congress president Subhankar Sarkar

title: Ritabrata camp of Trinamool Congress gets time till July 10 to submit response to EC notice
content: The EC had last week written to both factions of the Trinamool Congress, led by former West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee and Uluberia Purba MLA Ritabrata Banerjee, respectively, seeking responses regarding ‘claims and counterclaims’ made by them

title: Calcutta High Court to hear TMC Mamata Banerjee-led faction's petition for permission for rally on July 15
content: On July 10, a petiton was filed by Mamata Banerjee-led Trinamool Congress faction to seek direction to the police to allow its July 21 Martyrs' Day rally in front of Victoria House at Esplanade

title: Kerala Cabinet clears proposal to create Business Policy and Process Re-engineering Cell
content: In order to foster coordination among government departments and avoid delays in implementation of projects

title: SIR process focussed on exclusion, has played havoc with democracy: former CEC Quraishi
content: The ongoing process of Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls is "unfair" because of the way this is being done, said former Chief Election Commissioner S.Y. Quraishi

title: Mudragada’s family accuses Naidu of ‘harassment’, declines State honours for last rites
content: B. Kranthi, who had campaigned against her father’s party in 2024, was escorted away by the police amid slogans from family members and supporters at Kirlampudi

title: New voters have to also submit parents' SIR details for inclusion in electoral rolls
content: The functionary underlined that the declaration was added through instructions and Form 6 has not been amended

title: Delhi High Court asks Centre, Delhi govt. to respond to plea on Wangchuk’s deteriorating health
content: Delhi HC asks Centre, Delhi government to respond on the plea by tomorrow due to the ‘urgent’ nature of the issue

title: ‘When will PM Modi speak about people’s pain’: Congress slams government on inflation rise
content: Over the last 12 years of the Modi government, inflation has disrupted the budgets of ordinary families. Government data itself now reveals that retail inflation hit a 17-month high of 4.38% in June, said Jairam Ramesh

title: Congress expresses concern over resumption of hostilities in West Asia, urges Centre to protect India’s interests
content: Flagging Pakistan’s expanding role in West Asia, the Opposition party says the neighbouring country’s  growing influence in West Asia is a setback for India

title: Tamil Nadu Governor Arlekar, CM Vijay pay tribute to Kamaraj, Maraimalai Adigal on their birth anniversaries
content: Mr. Vijay, in a social media post, paid tribute to eminent Tamil scholar Maraimalai Adigal on his 150th birth anniversary

title: Kerala CM defends government pleader postings, says AG was consulted and competence assessed
content: Satheesan says the two names that were at the centre of the row over SFI and ABVP links were among the ones recommended by both Kerala committee and High Court unit of Indian Lawyers’ Congress

title: Cyberabad civic body directs Swiggy, Zomato to delist unlicensed food outlets, seeks details on customer review policy
content: The platforms were directed to verify and maintain the accurate geo-location of every listed food establishment and ensure that food is prepared and dispatched only from approved and registered premises

title: Where does your favourite Cyberabad restaurant rank? Check hygiene scores of 39 food establishments released by CMC
content: Cyberabad Municipal Corporation’s food safety teams assessed hygiene standards, food handling practices, pest control measures, storage conditions, labelling, record maintenance and compliance with FSSAI norms

title: Kerala high-speed rail project: CM Satheesan says govt panel finds Delhi Metro Rail Corporation proposal ‘incomplete’
content: Chief Minister says 'what the recommendations mean is that the State should not repeat the mistakes’ that occurred with the scrapped K-Rail project

title: Country heading towards economic crisis due to Modi government's policies: Congress
content: Congress demanded answers from Prime Minister Narendra Modi over the rising inflation, and said he alone must answer for this "economic failure"

title: Air India crash: Draft final report will be ready by October, AAIB tells Supreme Court
content: The affidavit stated that there is “an absolute statutory prohibition” on the public disclosure of cockpit voice recordings and airborne image recordings

title: Chembur tree fall incident: BMC’s report faces scrutiny over clean chit to officials
content: Urban forestry experts argue that the findings overlook the shared responsibility of civic agencies and that a one-size-fits-all clause is neither practical nor sufficient to prevent future tree collapses

title: 60 years after disappearing from Gir, Indian Grey Hornbills breed for fourth consecutive year
content: Study documents the successful establishment of the reintroduced population, with breeding recorded every year since the birds were released in 2021

title: Russia says it targeted Ukrainian ports overnight, four vessels hit
content: Russia’s Defence Ministry said targets in the ports of ​Odesa and Chornomorsk ‌in Ukraine’s Odesa region using precision-guided air-launched weapons and ‌attack drones

title: Chennai police arrest man for attempting to bribe Usilampatti TVK MLA Vijay
content: This case has been combined with an earlier case registered by the Triplicane police after N. Elaiyaraja, the Uthangarai MLA lodged a complaint with the Chennai Police Commissioner on June 29.

title: U.S. national held at Nepal border giving contradictory accounts, say officials
content: No passport or identity document was found on him and police recovered two mobile phones that appeared to have been ‘intentionally damaged’, a govt. official said

title: Karnataka government to fill management quota engineering seats of private and deemed universities through Karnataka Examinations Authority
content: There are a total of 28 private and 11 deemed universities in the State. While 37,690 engineering seats are available in private universities, 2,400 are available in deemed universities for 2026-27.

title: Kuwait fire: Two victims from Kerala’s Malappuram district
content: Bodies of M.P. Bahuleyan, 36, from Pulamanthol, and Nooh from Koottai, Tirur, were identified on June 12 night

title: MCC warns against littering, use of plastic atop Chamundi Hills during Ashada festivities in Mysuru
content: Pointing out that protecting the environment and maintaining cleanliness on Chamundi Hill is a collective responsibility, MCC urges organisations, volunteers, traders, and devotees to support its ‘Clean Chamundi Hill–Plastic-Free Ashada Month’ campaign

title: Two law students arrested for hurling abuses in Supreme Court, disrupting proceedings
content: According to police, the incident occurred during the hearing of Special Leave Petition (Civil), Prabal Pratap and Another vs State of Uttar Pradesh through Commissioner inside Court No. 13 of the apex court on July 10

title: What are the gains from the India-U.K. trade deal? | Explained
content: Zero tariffs. Cheaper whisky and British cars. Major benefits for Indian workers in the U.K. The India-U.K. trade deal could reshape business on both sides, and here’s the complete picture

title: Veteran Kapu leader Mudragada Padmanabham passes away at 73
content: An astute politician, Padmanabham had led Andhra Pradesh’s longest social movement to restore the Backward Class status for Kapus

title: West Bengal govt allocates 1,000 km of land to set up fence of about 172 km along international border
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said that his government has expedited the land transfer process to the Border Security Force (BSF) for the construction of essential fencing.

title: Matrimonial disputes: Kerala HC says courts must not assume bride entrusted gold based on social customs
content: HC says courts must look for oral, documentary or circumstantial evidence which can be used to infer if gold or money was entrusted to the husband or his relatives, or was misappropriated by them

title: Kejriwal to visit CJP protest at Jantar Mantar on July 16
content: "Sonam Wangchuk has been on a hunger strike for some days, and his health is deteriorating. He is an asset of the country, and I appeal to him to end his hunger strike. There are other ways also for the struggle," says Arvind Kejriwal

title: Bengaluru may get phased restrictions on entry-exit timings of heavy vehicles
content: The move, according to a BTP study, is expected to increase the average speed of vehicles in the city by at least 2 kmph

title: Phanindra Reddy appointed TNRERA Chairperson
content: The appointments come against the backdrop of the recent resignations of retired IAS officer Shiv Das Meena, who was serving as the Chairperson since August 2024, and a few other retired bureaucrats who had served as members.

title: ₹30,000 crore rural infrastructure boost: Odisha to build 50,000 km roads ahead of polls
content: In next five year, the massive investment in rural infrastructure includes Rs. 11,000 crore for 3837 bridges

title: RTI Chief Commissioner denied entry into Guntur police station during surprise inspection
content: Following the refusal, the CIC returned without inspecting the police station; he later continued his scheduled inspection by visiting the nearby MPDO offices

title: Oil exploration only after resolving Assam-Nagaland border disputes: Konyak Union
content: The apex body of the Konyak Nagas says territorial clarity, landowners’ consent, and stronger border security must precede implementation of the bilateral agreement

title: Law student arrested after fatal drunk driving crash in Odisha
content: A breath-analyser test conducted on accused finds 127 mg of alcohol per 100 ml of blood, more than four times the legal limit of 30 mg

title: For justice around the clock | Explained
content: The Supreme Court has decided to examine a writ petition for a framework to urgently set up Benches; the petition argues that in a system where executive power may operate at any hour, the protection of fundamental rights cannot remain constrained by the temporal boundaries of court schedules

title: Telangana’s new policy to strengthen healthcare infra in tribal areas: Health Minister
content: Damodar Raja Narasimha announces proposals to introduce CT scan services in all ITDA Area Hospitals, sanction of four more dialysis centres in Bhadradri Kothagudem and an ambulance for each mandal in the district.

title: Jaishankar meets European Council President, values his role in advancing trade, technology cooperation
content: The External Affairs Minister is on a two-day visit to Belgium from July 14 to 15 to participate in the 3rd India-EU Trade and Technology Council meeting and interact with his EU and Belgian counterparts in Brussels

title: India–UK CETA comes into force; Telangana well poised to reap benefits
content: Pharmaceuticals and Life Sciences, electronics and precision engineering, engineering goods and industrial machinery  and chemicals and specialty chemicals are set to benefit most from the landmark agreement that came into force on July 15

title: Goddess Saraswati without veena: Sculptures of Pallava, Chola eras reveal an earlier iconography
content: The veena, today regarded as inseparable from Saraswati’s identity, appears to have emerged as her dominant symbol only in the later period.

title: Karnataka will establish India’s first government-driven AI university, says Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar
content: Mr. Shivakumar said the proposed university would play a pivotal role in building world-class AI talent, advancing research and strengthening collaboration between academia, industry and government

title: Speculations abuzz after NCP, NCP SP leaders meet CM Fadnavis
content: Amid speculations that the majority of NCP SP legislators want to go with the BJP, the possibility of a merger between NCP and NCP SP is also being discussed in hush-hushed tones

title: Bihar CM Samrat Choudhary inaugurates State Level Sahyog Programme
content: Mr. Choudhary said the state government is working with a resolve to serve the public, and ensuring an impartial resolution for every grievance is the government’s top priority.

title: Jammu Family Takes Home Wrong Body, Missing Son Returns Alive Hours Later
content: The family's distress deepened the next morning when Vishal's sister in Punjab, received a call from the Government Medical College (GMC), Hospital about an unidentified body lying in the mortuary.

content: The raids were conducted at the residence of B Ravinder, the chief engineer of the Hyderabad Metropolitan Development Authority.

title: Opinion: Opinion | China Now Has 7 Of The World's 10 Biggest Banks, And That Should Worry India
content: Seven of the world's ten largest banks are now headquartered in China. Twenty-two Chinese banks figure in the top 100. No Indian bank comes anywhere close.

title: India-UK Trade Deal Kicks In: What Gets Cheaper, Which Sectors Gain Most
content: India-UK FTA: For Indian consumers, imported Scotch whisky, premium British cars, cosmetics, chocolates and select food products could get cheaper.

content: The case has sparked a political row, with opposition parties, including the DMK, criticising the state government over the alleged custodial death.

title: Out-Of-Control Honking: Dispute And Deepening Health And Safety Crisis
content: Recent violent incidents over road honking in India highlight severe noise pollution and health risks.

title: 8th Pay Commission: House Rent Allowance Hike May Be Bigger Than You Think
content: Since House Rent Allowance is directly linked to basic pay, any increase in the fitment factor could significantly raise the monthly allowance.

title: Army Calls For Peace After Attack On Assam Rifles In Nagaland Kills 1
content: The appeal came as Havildar Mohammad Iqbal of the 28 Assam Rifles Battalion was accorded full military honours at the Assam Rifles Training Centre and School in Dimapur.

title: Superhuman AI Just Years Away, Need Urgent Action Says Google AI Chief
content: Google AI boss Demis Hassabis says there is a small window before artificial general intelligence arrives and a global watchdog needs to be constituted urgently.

title: Can Bengaluru Start-Up's 'Virtual Magnet' Replace China's Rare-Earth Magnets?
content: Bengaluru-based start-up has invented 'Virtual Magnet' that allows them to build motors using only copper and steel, completely eliminating the need for rare-earth materials.

title: Taslima Nasreen Set To Return To Kolkata After 2 Decades, Row Erupts
content: Taslima Nasreen is scheduled to attend a programme protesting fundamentalism at Rabindra Sadan in Kolkata on August 1.

title: Navi Mumbai International Airport Launches First International Flight
content: Navi Mumbai Airport launches its first scheduled international flight to Abu Dhabi with Air India Express.

title: Gangster Rohit Godara's Home Razed Overnight, Cops Say 'We Didn't Do It'
content: Preliminary assessment suggested that an earthmover machine was used to bring down a considerable part of the building, officials said.

title: "Fastest Implementation": UK Trade Minister On Trade Deal With India
content: Chris Bryant said deal marks quickest turnaround UK has ever managed between signing an agreement and bringing it into effect.

title: Fake IAS Officer Assaults Husband, Demands 40 Lakhs To 'Build' Hospital
content: The accused posed as an IAS officer through fake social media to marry Abhishek. Later, strangulated him and demanded Rs 40 lakh as extortion.

title: UP Medical University Bans Cooking Non-Veg In Hostels After Governor Visit
content: KGMU bans preparation of non-vegetarian food in hostels after concerns raised by UP Governor Anandiben Patel.

title: On Run Since 2025, Criminal Posing As 'Peer Baba' Arrested By Hyderabad Cops
content: During the operation, police seized 115 grams of dry ganja (Cannabis) and the Peer Baba attire allegedly used by the accused to avoid detection.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: Iran Threatens To Shut More Energy Corridors After Hormuz Closure
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.
